message:
chore(beads): close br-r37-c1-fa5tf (link-prediction multigraph guards)

Fixed in commit 59980ee. cn_soundarajan_hopcroft,
ra_index_soundarajan_hopcroft, and within_inter_cluster now raise
NetworkXNotImplemented eagerly on multigraph/directed inputs
(restructured as inner generator returned from outer function so the
type check runs on call). Also bumped the within_inter_cluster
delta<=0 precondition to NetworkXAlgorithmError matching nx.

Sweep: 6774 passed, 0 failed (+13 new regression tests).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -65,7 +65,7 @@
 {"id":"br-r37-c1-f64cy","title":"parity: pagerank silently falls back to unweighted when weight attr is missing","description":"fnx.pagerank(G, weight='nope') on a graph whose edges lack a 'nope' attribute returns the unweighted result instead of raising. NetworkX defaults the missing attribute to 1 (treated as edge weight 1) — semantically same as unweighted but only when ALL edges lack the key. fnx behavior is similar but should be verified against nx for partial-attribute graphs (some edges weighted, others missing). Add parity test that builds a graph where half the edges have weight, half don't, and asserts fnx and nx agree to 1e-9.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-25T08:26:41.408497679Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-25T09:11:17.258068772Z","closed_at":"2026-04-25T09:11:17.256114832Z","close_reason":"Verified parity. fnx.pagerank already matches nx exactly on per-edge default-1 fallback for missing weight attributes — no code fix needed. Landed tests/python/test_pagerank_weight_parity.py (commit 400eefe) with 6 cases pinning the contract: all-weighted, weight='nope' (all missing), partial weights (half missing), weight=None, DiGraph partial, plus a sanity check that asymmetric weights produce a non-trivial pagerank delta vs unweighted (proving the implementation isn't in a hidden global fallback path).","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"br-r37-c1-f6epo","title":"parity: second_order_centrality dict iterates in wrong node order","description":"fnx.second_order_centrality returns a dict in arbitrary internal order (e.g. for path_graph(5): [2, 4, 0, 1, 3]) instead of nx's node-insertion order [0, 1, 2, 3, 4]. Same iteration-order drift as br-r37-c1-pm78h (communicability_betweenness_centrality). Drop-in code that iterates 'for n, score in centrality.items(): ...' breaks. Values are unchanged. Fix: reorder result dict in iteration order of G.nodes().","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-26T10:17:46.842301576Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-26T10:19:51.423520818Z","closed_at":"2026-04-26T10:19:51.421530578Z","close_reason":"Fixed in commit 58665d3. second_order_centrality now iterates in node-insertion order matching nx. 6 parity tests freeze the contract.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"br-r37-c1-f8gi1","title":"MultiGraph/MultiDiGraph.nodes() returns frozen list, not live NodeView","description":"fnx.MultiGraph.nodes() and fnx.MultiDiGraph.nodes() return a frozen list snapshot at call time. nx returns a live NodeView. Inconsistent with fnx.Graph.nodes() and fnx.DiGraph.nodes() which DO return live views.

Repro:
  >>> g = fnx.MultiGraph([(1,2)])
  >>> v = g.nodes()
  >>> g.add_node(99)
  >>> 99 in list(v)
  False                  # nx returns True

Root cause: the Rust-side MultiGraphNodeView.__call__ and MultiDiGraphNodeView.__call__ return list when called with default args (data=False). For Graph and DiGraph the Rust call returns self (a live NodeView) — only the multi-variants are wrong.

The Python wrapper _node_view_call_with_attr_support calls node_view_call(self, False, default) for data is False and trusts the Rust impl. Override that branch for Multi* classes to return self instead, matching the simple-graph branch.

Same class as br-r37-c1-msf5j (DiGraph/Multi*.edges() live view) — closes the parallel defect for nodes().","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-27T04:49:40.967497390Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-27T04:54:44.642938229Z","closed_at":"2026-04-27T04:54:44.640112396Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
-{"id":"br-r37-c1-fa5tf","title":"link-prediction soundarajan_hopcroft / within_inter_cluster raise wrong type on multigraph","description":"Three link-prediction functions raise NetworkXAlgorithmError('No community information available for Node 1') on multigraph input, masking the real issue. nx is decorated with @not_implemented_for('multigraph') and raises NetworkXNotImplemented FIRST, before any community-attribute check.

Repro:
  >>> g = fnx.MultiGraph([(1,2),(2,3)])
  >>> list(fnx.cn_soundarajan_hopcroft(g))
  NetworkXAlgorithmError: No community information available for Node 1
  >>> list(nx.cn_soundarajan_hopcroft(nx.MultiGraph([(1,2),(2,3)])))
  NetworkXNotImplemented: not implemented for multigraph type

Affects cn_soundarajan_hopcroft, ra_index_soundarajan_hopcroft, within_inter_cluster — all share the same code path that checks community attrs before the type guard. Drop-in code that does pytest.raises(NetworkXNotImplemented) won't trigger.

This is the link-prediction subset scoped out of br-r37-c1-tqimg. Fix: add multigraph guard at the top of each function, before any community-attribute lookup. Also add @not_implemented_for('directed') guards since nx decorates these functions with both.","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-27T08:14:21.278147577Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-27T08:14:52.279137656Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
+{"id":"br-r37-c1-fa5tf","title":"link-prediction soundarajan_hopcroft / within_inter_cluster raise wrong type on multigraph","description":"Three link-prediction functions raise NetworkXAlgorithmError('No community information available for Node 1') on multigraph input, masking the real issue. nx is decorated with @not_implemented_for('multigraph') and raises NetworkXNotImplemented FIRST, before any community-attribute check.

Repro:
  >>> g = fnx.MultiGraph([(1,2),(2,3)])
  >>> list(fnx.cn_soundarajan_hopcroft(g))
  NetworkXAlgorithmError: No community information available for Node 1
  >>> list(nx.cn_soundarajan_hopcroft(nx.MultiGraph([(1,2),(2,3)])))
  NetworkXNotImplemented: not implemented for multigraph type

Affects cn_soundarajan_hopcroft, ra_index_soundarajan_hopcroft, within_inter_cluster — all share the same code path that checks community attrs before the type guard. Drop-in code that does pytest.raises(NetworkXNotImplemented) won't trigger.

This is the link-prediction subset scoped out of br-r37-c1-tqimg. Fix: add multigraph guard at the top of each function, before any community-attribute lookup. Also add @not_implemented_for('directed') guards since nx decorates these functions with both.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-27T08:14:21.278147577Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-27T08:21:32.001176537Z","closed_at":"2026-04-27T08:21:31.998180181Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"br-r37-c1-fauol","title":"parity: ego_graph node ordering uses set iteration (random) instead of BFS-from-source order","description":"fnx.ego_graph node order is the iteration order of a set (arbitrary hash order). nx.ego_graph nodes are in BFS-from-source visit order (source first, then by distance from source). For node 'c' on graph with edges (c,d),(a,b),(b,c),(d,e), radius=2: fnx returns ['d','e','a','c','b'] but nx returns ['c','d','a','b','e']. Drop-in code that relies on iteration order breaks. Fix: iterate the BFS 'seen' dict (which records insertion order) instead of converting to set.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-26T11:07:07.265713125Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-26T11:10:10.734025927Z","closed_at":"2026-04-26T11:10:10.732030928Z","close_reason":"Fixed in commit 76c3369. ego_graph now iterates nodes in G's insertion order (filtered to ego set) matching nx's G.subgraph contract. 10 parity tests freeze the contract.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"br-r37-c1-fs3bl","title":"min_weight_matching tuple direction drifts from nx","description":"fnx.min_weight_matching returns matched pairs with different tuple directions than nx (same family pattern as br-r37-c1-kpnc8 which fixed max_weight_matching).

Repro:
  edges = [(0,1),(0,2),(1,2),(2,3)]
  fnx -> {(0,1), (2,3)}
  nx  -> {(1,0), (3,2)}   <- tuple direction reversed

  edges = [('c','d'),('a','b'),('b','c'),('d','e'),('a','c')]
  fnx -> {('a','c'), ('d','e')}
  nx  -> {('e','d'), ('a','c')}

The matching choice itself is correct (same nodes paired); only the per-pair tuple direction differs. Drop-in code that compared the matching against a reference set of (u,v) pairs broke.

Fix: delegate min_weight_matching to nx via _call_networkx_for_parity, mirroring br-r37-c1-kpnc8 (max_weight_matching). Preserve the existing weight= and MultiGraph-projection support.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-26T17:25:25.186247477Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-26T17:28:02.128625491Z","closed_at":"2026-04-26T17:28:02.126228636Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"br-r37-c1-fuuor","title":"parity: random_labeled_rooted_forest stray q arg + tutte_polynomial positional x/y","description":"Two stale signatures: random_labeled_rooted_forest(n, q=None, seed=None) carried a stray q from random_unlabeled_rooted_forest (nx is just (n, *, seed=None)). tutte_polynomial(G, x=None, y=None) accepted x/y positionally as a numeric-evaluation extension; nx is (G,) only. Drop-in callers writing for nx and trying tutte_polynomial(G, 1, 1) had the call work on fnx but raise TypeError on nx. Aligned: rrf drops q (replaced by random_unlabeled_rooted_forest forwarder), tutte_polynomial makes x/y KEYWORD_ONLY. Also fixed tutte_polynomial recursion to use kwarg form internally. 6 parity tests freeze the contract.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-26T04:36:37.664864574Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-26T04:37:22.476756359Z","closed_at":"2026-04-26T04:37:22.474915674Z","close_reason":"Fixed in commit 4b6c6de. random_labeled_rooted_forest signature is now (n, *, seed=None) matching nx; tutte_polynomial keeps the x/y numeric-evaluation extension but as KEYWORD_ONLY so positional usage rejects identically. 6 parity tests freeze the contract.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
